verdict_reasoning: VEGFA encodes a secreted, diffusible glycoprotein growth factor. It is synthesized with a signal peptide, processed through the secretory pathway, and released extracellularly. It binds VEGFR1/VEGFR2 on the surface of endothelial cells but this is transient non-covalent receptor engagement — it does not constitute wash-resistant surface anchoring of the VEGFA protein body itself. Checking each contextual bucket: (1) cell_state_induced — hypoxia induces secretion, not stable surface display of the protein body; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no lineage has documented stable VEGFA display on intact cells; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) dual_localization — no isoform is a stable TM precursor; the longest isoforms bind heparan sulfate proteoglycans in the matrix but this is matrix deposition, not PM-anchored display; (5) stable_surface_attachment — heparan sulfate binding is wash-sensitive and is matrix rather than PM anchoring. No antibody/ADC program targets VEGFA on the cell surface; anti-VEGF therapeutics (bevacizumab, aflibercept) target the soluble circulating form.
